_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Determine appropriate jurisdiction for legal action
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting cross-jurisdictional compliance mapping, identifying conflicts-of-law and overlapping requirements, and developing harmonized compliance approaches that satisfy multiple regulatory regimes simultaneously.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI RC domain. Skill RC-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-009 (RC, cosine: 0.431).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.5/10 (raw 0.348). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [action, appropriate, determine, jurisdiction, legal] vs WIDAI-only terms [approaches, compliance, conducting, conflicts, cross, developing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.844 (P=0.845, R=0.842). Balanced P/R — the 6-word NICE element and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Determine appropriate jurisdiction for legal action" and "Skill in conducting cross-jurisdictional compliance mapping, identifying conflic" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.501 > STS 0.348 (gap +0.153). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [action, appropriate, determine, jurisdiction, legal]. WIDAI terms: [approaches, compliance, conducting, conflicts, cross, developing].
